meta.description: AESOP Academy skill taxonomy and definitions with O*NET and WEF alignment

ai-fundamentals.title: AI Fundamentals & Machine Learning
ai-fundamentals.description: Core concepts of AI, machine learning paradigms, model training, and inference

prompt-engineering.title: Prompt Engineering & Interaction Design
prompt-engineering.description: Techniques for crafting effective prompts and designing multi-turn interactions with AI systems

generative-ai.title: Large Language Models & Generative AI
generative-ai.description: Understanding LLM architecture, capabilities, fine-tuning, and generative AI applications

ai-applications.title: AI Applications & Problem-Solving
ai-applications.description: Applying AI to solve real-world problems across domains; selecting appropriate tools and models

ai-ethics-governance.title: AI Governance & Ethics
ai-ethics-governance.description: Understanding responsible AI, governance frameworks, bias mitigation, and ethical decision-making

ai-society.title: AI in Society & Impact
ai-society.description: Understanding AI's societal implications, economic impact, job displacement, and human-AI collaboration

ai-systems-agents.title: Building AI Systems & Agents
ai-systems-agents.description: Designing and implementing AI systems, agents, orchestration, and multi-step AI workflows

rag-knowledge-systems.title: RAG & Knowledge Systems
rag-knowledge-systems.description: Building retrieval-augmented generation systems, vector databases, and knowledge management

ai-security.title: AI Security & Safety
ai-security.description: Securing AI systems, detecting adversarial attacks, ensuring model safety and reliability

data-analytics.title: Data Science & Analytics
data-analytics.description: Data collection, preparation, analysis, and deriving insights using statistical and ML methods

understand-ai-basics.title: Understand AI Basics
understand-ai-basics.description: Comprehend foundational AI concepts: ML paradigms, model training, inference, neural networks, and basic AI terminology

relevantActivities: Develop machine learning models using statistical techniques
Determine AI training requirements and data needs
onet.notes: Foundational AI knowledge required for data science and ML engineering roles

wef.notes: WEF ranks analytical thinking and technological literacy as top 5 skills for 2024-2028

grasp-llm-behavior.title: Grasp LLM Behavior & Capabilities
grasp-llm-behavior.description: Understand how large language models work, what they can and cannot do, token limits, context windows, and model variations

relevantActivities: Evaluate model performance and behavior characteristics
Understand algorithm capabilities and limitations
onet.notes: Essential for roles working with LLMs and generative AI systems

wef.notes: Critical thinking about technology capabilities is increasingly important for knowledge work

recognize-ai-limitations.title: Recognize AI Limitations & Failure Modes
recognize-ai-limitations.description: Identify AI failure modes, hallucinations, biases, edge cases, and understand when not to use AI

relevantActivities: Test model reliability and edge cases
Identify risks and failure scenarios
onet.notes: Critical for responsible AI deployment and risk assessment

wef.notes: Risk awareness and critical evaluation of AI systems increasingly valued

write-clear-prompts.title: Write Clear, Effective Prompts
write-clear-prompts.description: Craft single-turn and basic multi-turn prompts with clarity, context, and expected output formats

relevantActivities: Communicate technical requirements clearly
Design user interaction flows
onet.notes: Prompt writing is a new core skill for AI-augmented development roles

wef.notes: Prompt engineering emerging as fundamental skill for AI-era knowledge workers

design-multi-turn-conversations.title: Design Multi-Turn AI Conversations
design-multi-turn-conversations.description: Structure extended dialogues with AI systems, maintain context, refine outputs iteratively, and manage conversation memory

relevantActivities: Design conversational user interfaces
Implement chat and dialogue systems
onet.notes: Multi-turn conversation design essential for conversational AI applications

wef.notes: Conversational design increasingly central to user-facing AI applications

leverage-system-prompts.title: Leverage System Prompts & Instructions
leverage-system-prompts.description: Use system-level prompts, role definitions, and instruction-tuning to shape AI behavior and constrain outputs

relevantActivities: Configure and tune AI system behavior
Define AI system constraints and safety rails
onet.notes: Advanced prompt engineering for production AI system control

wef.notes: Fine-grained control of AI behavior increasingly important for governance

optimize-output-formats.title: Optimize AI Output Formats
optimize-output-formats.description: Request and format AI outputs for specific use cases: JSON, markdown, code, structured data, etc.

relevantActivities: Integrate AI outputs into software systems
Parse and process structured AI responses
onet.notes: Critical for integrating AI into production systems

wef.notes: Technical skill for AI system integration

understand-llm-architecture.title: Understand LLM Architecture & Training
understand-llm-architecture.description: Comprehend transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, training processes, and model scaling principles

relevantActivities: Study and understand deep learning architectures
Analyze neural network mechanisms
onet.notes: Deep technical knowledge required for advanced ML/AI roles

wef.notes: Deep technical understanding of LLMs increasingly valued in tech roles

work-with-embeddings.title: Work with Embeddings & Vector Representations
work-with-embeddings.description: Understand embeddings, vector spaces, similarity search, and use embeddings in applications

relevantActivities: Work with vector representations and semantic search
Implement embedding-based systems
onet.notes: Essential for modern vector database and RAG applications

wef.notes: Vector techniques foundational to modern AI systems

fine-tune-language-models.title: Fine-Tune Language Models
fine-tune-language-models.description: Adapt pre-trained LLMs to specific domains through fine-tuning, LoRA, and instruction-tuning

relevantActivities: Adapt and customize machine learning models
Optimize models for specific applications
onet.notes: Advanced skill for customized AI systems and model optimization

wef.notes: Model customization increasingly important for competitive advantage

use-generative-ai-tools.title: Use Generative AI Tools & APIs
use-generative-ai-tools.description: Leverage generative AI platforms (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) and integrate them into applications

relevantActivities: Integrate generative AI APIs into applications
Implement AI-powered features
onet.notes: Practical skill for most modern software development roles

wef.notes: Practical AI tool use becoming baseline expectation across roles

identify-ai-use-cases.title: Identify AI Use Cases & Opportunities
identify-ai-use-cases.description: Recognize where AI can add value, evaluate AI solution fit for business problems, and scope AI projects

relevantActivities: Identify opportunities for AI automation and improvement
Evaluate AI solutions for business needs
onet.notes: Critical skill for AI leadership and project scoping roles

wef.notes: AI opportunity identification emerging as essential leadership competency

solve-problems-with-ai.title: Solve Real-World Problems with AI
solve-problems-with-ai.description: Apply AI techniques to analyze problems, develop solutions, and create AI-powered products or services

relevantActivities: Develop software solutions incorporating AI
Apply AI to solve application-specific challenges
onet.notes: Practical problem-solving with AI is core competency for tech roles

wef.notes: Complex problem-solving with technology tools top WEF skill for 2024+

evaluate-ai-solutions.title: Evaluate & Compare AI Solutions
evaluate-ai-solutions.description: Assess AI solutions based on accuracy, cost, latency, bias, and alignment with requirements

relevantActivities: Evaluate technology solutions and trade-offs
Make architecture and tool selection decisions
onet.notes: Critical for technical leadership and procurement decisions

wef.notes: Analytical evaluation of AI solutions key for responsible deployment

understand-ai-ethics.title: Understand AI Ethics & Responsible AI
understand-ai-ethics.description: Grasp ethical principles, responsible AI frameworks, fairness, transparency, and accountability in AI systems

relevantActivities: Consider ethical implications of AI systems
Implement fairness and accountability measures
onet.notes: Ethics increasingly required competency for AI roles

wef.notes: WEF identifies ethical AI and responsible business as top future skill areas

identify-ai-bias.title: Identify & Mitigate AI Bias
identify-ai-bias.description: Recognize sources of bias in data and models, conduct fairness analysis, and implement bias mitigation strategies

relevantActivities: Analyze data for bias and fairness issues
Implement bias detection and mitigation techniques
onet.notes: Bias detection becoming critical requirement for AI system development

wef.notes: Fairness and bias mitigation increasingly mandated by regulations and policy

apply-governance-frameworks.title: Apply AI Governance Frameworks
apply-governance-frameworks.description: Implement governance structures, policies, and controls for responsible AI development and deployment

relevantActivities: Establish policies and governance for technology systems
Ensure compliance with regulations and standards
onet.notes: Governance and compliance increasingly central to AI leadership roles

wef.notes: AI governance frameworks essential for organizational readiness

ensure-responsible-ai.title: Ensure Responsible AI Deployment
ensure-responsible-ai.description: Design and implement responsible AI practices: testing, monitoring, safety rails, and continuous improvement

relevantActivities: Implement safety and reliability measures in AI systems
Test and validate AI system behavior in production
onet.notes: Responsible deployment practices critical for production AI systems

wef.notes: Safety and responsibility increasingly non-negotiable for AI systems

understand-ai-societal-impact.title: Understand AI's Societal Impact
understand-ai-societal-impact.description: Grasp AI's effects on employment, inequality, education, and society; understand economic and policy implications

relevantActivities: Educate others about AI implications
Discuss AI impact on society and work
onet.notes: Societal literacy about AI increasingly expected across roles

wef.notes: Societal understanding of technology increasingly important for leadership

navigate-ai-driven-change.title: Navigate AI-Driven Workplace Change
navigate-ai-driven-change.description: Adapt to AI-augmented workflows, learn new AI tools, understand job transformation, and build AI-era skills

relevantActivities: Lead organizational adaptation to new technologies
Support workforce transition and reskilling
onet.notes: Change management and adaptation essential for AI-era leadership

wef.notes: WEF identifies resilience, flexibility, and lifelong learning as top 2024+ skills

communicate-ai-concepts.title: Communicate AI Concepts to Diverse Audiences
communicate-ai-concepts.description: Explain AI concepts clearly to technical and non-technical audiences; demystify AI and build shared understanding

relevantActivities: Communicate technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders
Build organizational AI literacy
onet.notes: Communication increasingly critical for technical leadership

wef.notes: Communication and collaboration top 10 WEF skills for 2024-2028

design-ai-agents.title: Design AI Agents & Orchestration
design-ai-agents.description: Architect multi-agent systems, design agent interactions, manage orchestration and tool use

relevantActivities: Design complex AI system architectures
Implement multi-component AI solutions
onet.notes: AI agent design emerging as critical software architecture skill

wef.notes: Advanced AI system design increasingly valuable for competitive advantage

integrate-multiple-ai-models.title: Integrate Multiple AI Models
integrate-multiple-ai-models.description: Combine different AI models and systems, implement model routing and selection logic, manage model composition

relevantActivities: Integrate multiple AI/ML components
Manage complex AI system interactions
onet.notes: Model integration and composition key skill for advanced AI systems

wef.notes: Systems integration and composition increasingly important

implement-ai-workflows.title: Implement AI Workflows & Automation
implement-ai-workflows.description: Create multi-step AI workflows, implement automation pipelines, and orchestrate AI-augmented business processes

relevantActivities: Implement workflow automation with AI
Design AI-powered business process automation
onet.notes: Workflow automation with AI becoming standard development practice

wef.notes: AI-augmented automation core to future work productivity

manage-agent-memory-context.title: Manage Agent Memory & Context
manage-agent-memory-context.description: Design agent memory systems, manage context windows, implement long-term memory, and maintain conversation state

relevantActivities: Manage persistent state in AI systems
Implement memory and knowledge management for agents
onet.notes: Memory management critical for stateful, multi-turn agent systems

wef.notes: Memory and context management advanced technical skill

build-rag-systems.title: Build Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Systems
build-rag-systems.description: Design and implement RAG systems, connect knowledge sources to generative models, and optimize retrieval

relevantActivities: Implement knowledge retrieval systems
Build systems that augment AI with external knowledge
onet.notes: RAG becoming standard pattern for AI-augmented systems

wef.notes: RAG systems increasingly central to enterprise AI deployment

work-with-vector-databases.title: Work with Vector Databases
work-with-vector-databases.description: Use vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant) for semantic search, similarity search, and knowledge management

relevantActivities: Implement vector search and retrieval systems
Work with specialized databases for AI applications
onet.notes: Vector database expertise increasingly critical for modern data applications

wef.notes: Vector database expertise valuable for data engineering and ML

design-knowledge-graphs.title: Design & Implement Knowledge Graphs
design-knowledge-graphs.description: Model domain knowledge as graphs, implement graph databases, and use for reasoning and knowledge representation

relevantActivities: Design structured knowledge representations
Implement semantic and knowledge management systems
onet.notes: Knowledge graphs increasingly important for semantic AI systems

wef.notes: Knowledge representation and reasoning valued in AI systems

secure-ai-systems.title: Secure AI Systems & Implementations
secure-ai-systems.description: Implement security controls for AI systems, protect against misuse, implement access controls, and secure APIs

relevantActivities: Implement security measures for AI systems
Protect AI systems from unauthorized access and misuse
onet.notes: AI security expertise increasingly critical as systems proliferate

wef.notes: WEF identifies cybersecurity and networks as top tech skills for 2024+

detect-ai-vulnerabilities.title: Detect AI Vulnerabilities & Attacks
detect-ai-vulnerabilities.description: Identify prompt injection attacks, adversarial examples, model extraction, and other AI-specific vulnerabilities

relevantActivities: Identify vulnerabilities in AI systems
Test and audit AI systems for security flaws
onet.notes: AI-specific threat detection becoming critical security skill

wef.notes: Security of AI systems increasingly regulatory and business-critical

implement-safety-measures.title: Implement AI Safety Measures
implement-safety-measures.description: Design safety constraints, implement guard rails, test for harmful outputs, and ensure AI alignment

relevantActivities: Implement safety systems and constraints
Design and test alignment measures
onet.notes: AI safety increasingly important for responsible system deployment

wef.notes: Safety and responsible operation critical for AI-era development

collect-and-prepare-data.title: Collect & Prepare Data for AI
collect-and-prepare-data.description: Gather data from sources, clean and validate data, handle missing values, and prepare datasets for analysis

relevantActivities: Collect and manage data from multiple sources
Clean, validate, and prepare data for analysis
onet.notes: Data preparation is foundational skill for all data science and AI roles

wef.notes: Data fluency increasingly important across all roles

perform-data-analysis.title: Perform Data Analysis & Visualization
perform-data-analysis.description: Analyze datasets using statistical methods, create visualizations, identify patterns, and derive insights

relevantActivities: Analyze data to identify patterns and insights
Communicate data findings through visualization
onet.notes: Data analysis and interpretation critical for data-driven decision making

wef.notes: Analytical skills and data interpretation top WEF skills for 2024+

build-predictive-models.title: Build & Deploy Predictive Models
build-predictive-models.description: Train machine learning models, evaluate model performance, optimize parameters, and deploy for prediction

relevantActivities: Develop machine learning models for prediction
Evaluate and optimize model performance
Deploy models to production
onet.notes: Predictive modeling core competency for data science and ML roles

wef.notes: ML and predictive analytics increasingly central to data roles
